title: Thailand Street Food — 25 Must-Try Dishes & Prices (2026)
meta_description: The 25 best Thai street food dishes with real 2026 prices. From pad thai to mango sticky rice — what to eat, what it costs, and where to find it.
intro: After years of eating my way through Bangkok's sois, Chiang Mai's night markets, and Isan's roadside grills, I've narrowed down the dishes every visitor should try. These aren't restaurant recommendations — this is the food you'll find on plastic tables under fluorescent lights, cooked by vendors who've been perfecting one dish for decades. All prices are real 2025-2026 street vendor prices. Tourist areas like Khao San Road charge 50-100% more.

items.name: Pad Thai
items.description: Stir-fried rice noodles with egg, tofu, tamarind sauce, fish sauce, dried shrimp, and bean sprouts, garnished with crushed peanuts and lime. Protein options include shrimp, chicken, or pork. Thailand's most internationally recognized dish.
items.where_to_find: Thip Samai on Maha Chai Road (famous 'Pad Thai Pratu Pi'), Yaowarat (Chinatown), Hoi Tod Chaw Lae on Ekkamai
key_facts: Thailand's most famous dish worldwide
Thip Samai has been open since 1966
Local price 40-60 THB, tourist areas 100-120 THB

items.name: Pad Kra Pao
items.description: Minced meat stir-fried with holy basil, garlic, chilies, fish sauce, and oyster sauce, served over rice with a fried egg (kai dao) on top. What Thais actually eat daily — arguably the real national dish. Holy basil (kra pao) is different from sweet basil (horapha).
items.where_to_find: Ung Jia Huad near Asoke (47 years running, ~50 THB), Phed Mark by Mark Wiens near BTS Ekkamai (5 spice levels)
key_facts: Thailand's true daily staple
Order 'kai dao' for a fried egg on top
Phed Mark offers 5 spice levels

items.name: Som Tam
items.description: Shredded green papaya pounded in a mortar with tomatoes, long beans, dried shrimp, peanuts, garlic, chilies, fish sauce, lime juice, and palm sugar. An Isan (northeastern) staple with dozens of variations: som tam thai (mild, peanuts), som tam poo (raw crab), som tam pla ra (fermented fish).
items.where_to_find: Isan food clusters around Victory Monument, Bang Rak area, every street corner in Thailand
key_facts: Tell vendor how many chilies you want
Part of the Isan trio: som tam + gai yang + sticky rice
Som tam pla ra is the most intense version

items.name: Tom Yum Goong
items.description: Hot and sour soup with shrimp, lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, mushrooms, fish sauce, and fresh chilies. Comes in clear (nam sai) or creamy (nam khon) versions. Added to UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage list.
items.where_to_find: Mit Ko Yuan on Dinso Road (90 THB), Tom Yum Goong Banglamphu near Khao San, T&K Seafood on Yaowarat
key_facts: UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage
Creamy version uses nam prik pao (roasted chili paste)
Mit Ko Yuan charges ~90 THB
items.google_maps_query: Mit Ko Yuan Tom Yum Bangkok

items.name: Mango Sticky Rice
items.thai_name: ข้าวเหนียวมะม่วง
items.description: Sweet sticky rice soaked in coconut milk, served with slices of ripe Nam Dok Mai mango and drizzled with additional coconut cream. Best during mango season (April-June) when the fruit is at its sweetest and cheapest. Off-season prices rise and quality drops.
items.where_to_find: Mae Varee near BTS Thong Lo (famous vendor), Chatuchak Market, night markets everywhere
key_facts: Best in April-June (mango season)
Mae Varee at Thong Lo is the most famous vendor
Thailand's most famous dessert worldwide
items.google_maps_query: Mae Varee Mango Sticky Rice Bangkok

items.name: Khao Soi
items.description: Northern Thai curry noodle soup with egg noodles in a rich coconut curry broth, topped with crispy fried noodles, pickled mustard greens, shallots, and lime. Usually with chicken or beef. Burmese-influenced and almost exclusively a northern dish.
items.where_to_find: Khao Soi Khun Yai (Grandma's Khao Soi), Khao Soi Lung Prakit Kad Kom (Michelin Bib Gourmand), Khao Soi Mae Sai (Michelin Bib Gourmand 2026)
key_facts: Undisputed Chiang Mai specialty
Crispy noodle topping is signature
Two Michelin Bib Gourmand winners in Chiang Mai
items.google_maps_query: Khao Soi Khun Yai Chiang Mai

items.name: Moo Ping
items.description: Marinated grilled pork skewers — pork strips in garlic, coriander root, fish sauce, soy sauce, coconut milk, and palm sugar, grilled over charcoal. Sold from 5 AM at morning markets, usually in sets of 3-4 sticks with sticky rice (30-50 THB). Thailand's quintessential breakfast food.
items.where_to_find: Moo Ping Hea Owen in Silom/Sathorn (sells 2,000+ sticks per hour), morning markets everywhere
key_facts: Thailand's grab-and-go breakfast
Moo Ping Hea Owen sells 2,000+ sticks/hour
3-4 sticks + sticky rice = 30-50 THB
items.google_maps_query: Moo Ping Hea Owen Silom Bangkok

items.name: Khao Mun Gai
items.thai_name: ข้าวมันไก่
items.description: Thai-style Hainanese chicken rice — poached chicken served over rice cooked in chicken fat and broth, with a bowl of clear chicken soup on the side. Accompanied by a special sauce of fermented soybean, ginger, vinegar, and chili. One of the mildest, most approachable Thai dishes for newcomers.
items.where_to_find: Go-Ang Pratunam Chicken Rice (Michelin Bib Gourmand, 'pink restaurant'), Boon Tong Kiat on Soi Polo
key_facts: Go-Ang Pratunam = Michelin Bib Gourmand
Known as the 'pink restaurant' for staff uniforms
Perfect for spice-sensitive travelers

items.name: Boat Noodles
items.thai_name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ
items.description: Intensely flavored noodle soup with dark broth containing soy sauces, herbs, cinnamon, and star anise. Traditionally tiny portions — you order 5-10 bowls and stack them. Originally sold from boats on Bangkok's canals. Ask for 'nam tok' with pig blood for richness, or 'mai nam tok' without.
items.where_to_find: Victory Monument boat noodle alley (most famous cluster), Saen Saeb canal area
key_facts: 10-15 THB per tiny bowl — order 5-10
Stack empty bowls to keep count
Originally sold from boats on canals
items.google_maps_query: Victory Monument Boat Noodle Alley Bangkok

items.name: Gai Yang
items.description: Whole or half chicken marinated in garlic, coriander root, lemongrass, turmeric, soy sauce, and fish sauce, grilled slowly over charcoal. Served with sticky rice and nam jim jaew (spicy dipping sauce). Part of the classic Isan trio with som tam and sticky rice.
items.where_to_find: Sabai Jai Gai Yang at Ekkamai (150-250 THB full meal), Gai Yang Klang Bueng, SPE Gai Yang on Convent Road
key_facts: Classic Isan trio: gai yang + som tam + sticky rice
SPE Gai Yang on Convent Road is an institution
Street half-chicken 40-80 THB, restaurant meals 150-250 THB
items.google_maps_query: Sabai Jai Gai Yang Ekkamai Bangkok

items.name: Satay
items.description: Thin slices of pork, chicken, or beef marinated in turmeric, coconut milk, coriander, and cumin, skewered and grilled over charcoal. Served with peanut dipping sauce, cucumber relish, and sometimes grilled toast. Thai-Muslim/Malay origin.
items.where_to_find: Dee Moo Satay on Tha Din Daeng Road near Wongwian Yai (7,000-8,000 sticks daily), Victory Monument area
key_facts: Dee Moo Satay's grill is 'longer than a school bus'
Sells 7,000-8,000 sticks daily
Sold in bundles of 10-20 sticks

items.name: Guay Teow
items.thai_name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยว
items.description: Thai noodle soup — choose your noodle width (sen mee/thin, sen lek/medium, sen yai/wide, ba mee/egg), broth (clear, tom yum, or nam tok/dark), and protein (pork, chicken, beef, fish balls). The condiment caddy with 4 jars (sugar, chili flakes, fish sauce, vinegar) is a signature experience.
items.where_to_find: Every soi and market in Thailand — look for noodle carts with glass cases of ingredients
key_facts: Thais always customize with the 4-jar condiment caddy
Choose your noodle width and broth style
The most eaten everyday dish in Thailand

items.name: Khao Pad
items.description: Thai fried rice with egg, onion, and your choice of protein (shrimp, chicken, pork, crab). Flavored with fish sauce, soy sauce, and a squeeze of lime. Sometimes served in a hollowed-out pineapple (khao pad sapparod). One of the safest bets for newcomers.
items.where_to_find: Everywhere — Yaowarat, Victory Monument, Silom, Chatuchak Market
key_facts: Safest bet for picky eaters
Pineapple version is a Thai-Chinese fusion
Ask for 'prik nam pla' (chili fish sauce) on the side

items.name: Thai Iced Tea
items.description: Strong brewed Ceylon tea mixed with sugar and condensed/evaporated milk, poured over crushed ice. The distinctive bright orange color comes from food coloring or star anise. Also available as cha dum yen (black tea without milk).
items.where_to_find: Every street vendor, market, and food court in Thailand
key_facts: Iconic orange color
Street price 25-35 THB, cafes 60-80 THB
Ask for 'cha dum yen' for the black tea version
items.google_maps_query: Thai Iced Tea street vendor Bangkok

items.name: Hoy Tod
items.description: Crispy oyster or mussel omelette — fresh shellfish cooked in a batter of rice flour and tapioca starch with egg and bean sprouts. Available in crispy (tod krop) or soft versions. Served with sweet chili sauce.
items.where_to_find: Nai Mong Hoy Tod in Yaowarat/Chinatown (70-100 THB, 30+ years), Wang Lang Market (De Yai Chuan Cheem)
key_facts: Nai Mong Hoy Tod is the gold standard
Ask for 'tod krop' for extra crispy
30+ year-old institution on Yaowarat
items.google_maps_query: Nai Mong Hoy Tod Yaowarat Bangkok

items.name: Roti
items.description: Thin flatbread fried on a hot griddle with butter, folded with fillings like banana, egg, Nutella, or condensed milk. Crispy outside, soft inside. Brought to Thailand by Muslim Indian traders — southern Thailand (Krabi, Phuket) has the best roti due to stronger Muslim heritage.
items.where_to_find: Khao San Road, Phuket Naka Weekend Market, night markets everywhere
key_facts: Thai-Muslim origin via Indian traders
Banana + condensed milk is the classic combo
Southern Thailand makes the best roti

items.name: Nam Tok Moo
items.description: 'Waterfall pork' — grilled pork sliced thin and tossed with toasted rice powder (khao khua), shallots, fresh mint, cilantro, green onions, dried chili flakes, fish sauce, and lime juice. Served with sticky rice and raw cabbage. The toasted rice powder adds a distinctive nutty, smoky crunch.
items.where_to_find: Isan food stalls and som tam vendors (they almost always serve nam tok too), Victory Monument area
key_facts: 'Nam tok' means waterfall — refers to juices dripping from the grill
Toasted rice powder (khao khua) is the secret ingredient
Always served with som tam at Isan food stalls

items.name: Coconut Ice Cream
items.thai_name: ไอศครีมกะทิ
items.description: Fresh coconut milk ice cream served in a coconut shell with toppings like peanuts, corn, palm seeds, sticky rice, red beans, coco jelly, and mung beans. Real coconut ice cream has no dairy — naturally vegan.
items.where_to_find: Coco JJ at Chatuchak Market (40 THB base + 20 THB for 2 toppings), night markets, floating markets
key_facts: Coco JJ at Chatuchak is Instagram-famous
Naturally dairy-free and vegan
9 toppings available at Coco JJ

items.name: Sai Krok Isan
items.thai_name: ไส้กรอกอีสาน
items.description: Fermented pork and sticky rice sausage from northeastern Thailand. The 3-4 day fermentation gives it a distinctive sour, tangy flavor. Grilled over charcoal until crispy outside. Served with raw bird's eye chilies, sliced ginger, and fresh cabbage.
items.where_to_find: Isan food stalls at any market, Victory Monument area, morning and evening markets
key_facts: Fermented 3-4 days for sour taste
Look for round, linked sausages on the grill
Mild sausage, hot condiments on the side

items.name: Sai Oua
items.description: Northern Thai spicy sausage made with minced pork, red curry paste, lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves, galangal, and fresh herbs. More aromatic and herbal than Sai Krok Isan. A distinctly northern dish rarely found outside Chiang Mai.
items.where_to_find: Chiang Mai Gate night market, Warorot Market (Kad Luang) in Chiang Mai, northern Thai food stalls
key_facts: Exclusively northern Thai
Fresh and herbal vs Sai Krok Isan's sour fermented taste
Chiang Mai Gate market is the best spot

items.name: Kanom Jeen
items.description: Fresh fermented rice noodles served with various curry toppings. Popular versions: nam ya (fish curry, southern), nam ngiew (pork and tomato, northern), green curry chicken (central). Noodles are fermented 3 days before being extruded through a sieve into boiling water.
items.where_to_find: Kanom Jeen Buffet Chao Din at MBK Mall 5th floor (59 THB all-you-can-eat), morning markets
key_facts: Each region has its own curry pairing
MBK Mall has all-you-can-eat for 59 THB
Noodles fermented 3 days

items.name: Khanom Buang
items.description: Thai crispy crepes — thin taco-shaped shells filled with meringue cream and topped with either sweet (shredded coconut, foi thong egg yolk strips) or savory (chopped scallions, shrimp) toppings. One of Thailand's oldest street snacks, dating back centuries.
items.where_to_find: Khanom Buang Phraeng Nara in Phra Nakhon, Damnoen Saduak Floating Market, near temples and markets
key_facts: One of Thailand's oldest street snacks
Sweet and savory versions equally popular
10-35 THB per set

items.name: Lod Chong
items.description: Green pandan-flavored rice flour noodles served in sweetened coconut milk with crushed ice. The green 'worms' are made from rice flour, mung bean flour, and pandan juice, pressed through a sieve. Topped with palm sugar syrup. A perfect heat-beating dessert.
items.where_to_find: Any Thai dessert vendor (carts with colorful sweet options), temple fairs, food courts
key_facts: Pandan gives green color and floral aroma
Also known as cendol in Malaysia
Perfect for cooling down in the heat

items.name: Fresh Fruit Shakes
items.thai_name: น้ำผลไม้ปั่น
items.description: Freshly blended tropical fruit smoothies made to order — mango, papaya, watermelon, pineapple, passion fruit, or mixed. Blended with ice and often sweetened with sugar or condensed milk. Say 'mai sai nam tan' for no added sugar.
items.where_to_find: Every market, beach, and street corner — Ratchada Train Night Market has a huge selection
key_facts: Ask 'mai sai nam tan' for no sugar
Choose vendors that peel fruit fresh
Mango shakes are the tourist favorite

items.name: Oliang
items.description: Traditional Thai iced coffee brewed from Robusta beans mixed with corn, soybeans, sesame seeds, and cardamom. Brewed through a cloth filter (tungdtom), sweetened with sugar and condensed milk, served over ice. Street vendors still use the traditional cloth bag method.
items.where_to_find: Morning market coffee carts, street vendors throughout Bangkok
key_facts: Traditional cloth bag (tungdtom) brewing
Robusta beans for stronger, more bitter flavor
15-30 THB street price, up to 60 THB in cafes

tips: A full day of street food costs 150-300 THB ($4.50-$9 USD) in local neighborhoods. Tourist zones like Khao San Road charge 50-100% more for the same dishes.
Point and order — most vendors display their food or have pictures. Saying 'ao nee' (I want this) while pointing works perfectly.
Tell som tam vendors how many chilies you want: 'prik song met' (2 chilies) for mild, or 'mai sai prik' for no chili at all.
Morning markets (6-11 AM) are for moo ping, khao mun gai, and kanom jeen. Night markets (5-11 PM) have the widest variety of everything else.
Look for long queues of Thai customers — they know which vendors make the best version. Tourist-facing stalls with English menus often charge more and taste worse.
Yaowarat (Chinatown) and Victory Monument are Bangkok's two best street food zones. Yaowarat for seafood and Chinese-Thai dishes, Victory Monument for Isan and boat noodles.
The 4-jar condiment set at noodle stalls is meant to be used: sugar (sweetness), chili flakes (heat), fish sauce (saltiness), and vinegar with chilies (sourness). Balance all four.
Sticky rice is eaten with your hands — pinch off a small ball, press it flat, and use it to scoop up meat or dip into sauce. Don't use a fork.
Food inflation in 2026 sits at roughly 0.92% year-over-year. Street food remains one of the world's best food bargains.
